claims_by_chunk.topic: Kepler's First Law and Orbit Parameters
claims: Satellites orbit the Earth following an elliptical trajectory with Earth located at one focal point.
The semi-major axis of an orbit determines the average orbital distance from the Earth's center.
Eccentricity defines the shape of an orbit, ranging from circular to highly elliptical.
A circular orbit is achieved when the eccentricity equals zero.
